summary: Fair‑quality audience tape of a classic 1970 New York show, highlighted by stellar Deep Elem Blues, Morning Dew and an soaring Attics of My Life despite the rough sound.
review: The performance shines with deep blues numbers like Deep Elem Blues and Morning Dew, plus a gorgeous rendition of Attics of My Life that many reviewers call one of the best ever. Recording quality is uneven – some tracks are listenable while others are described as wretched or muddy – but the musical moments still make it worthwhile for collectors.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is praised by many reviewers (standout songs, strong performances) while recording quality is repeatedly criticized as poor or variable; weighting show (70%) and recording (30%) yields a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviews describe the tape as an old audience (AUD) source with "mudeled 10th generation board" quality, often wretched or muddy, varying greatly between tracks; some listeners find it listenable on portions but overall the sound is uneven and noisy.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical early‑70s New York set that swings through blues, folk and tight harmonies; reviewers note strong flow despite the recording issues.

band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for jackhammer‑like bass work on Deep Elem Blues ("Phil was the leader instrumentally").
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for a great second guitar contribution from young Robert Weir on Deep Elem Blues.
